title: Tyranny and Oppression
subtitle: Darkness upon Darkness on the Day of Judgment

verses.arabic: إِنَّمَا السَّبِيلُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَظْلِمُونَ النَّاسَ وَيَبْغُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
verses.translation: The cause is only against the ones who wrong people and tyrannize upon the earth without right. Those will have a painful punishment.

verses.arabic: وَأَنذِرِ النَّاسَ يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمُ الْعَذَابُ فَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا رَبَّنَا أَخِّرْنَا إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ
verses.translation: And warn the people of a Day when the punishment will come to them and those who wronged will say, 'Our Lord, delay us for a short term.'

verses.arabic: وَسَيَعْلَمُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا أَيَّ مُنقَلَبٍ يَنقَلِبُونَ
verses.translation: And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.

verses.arabic: كَانُوا لَا يَتَنَاهَوْنَ عَن مُّنكَرٍ فَعَلُوهُ ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ
verses.translation: They used not to prevent one another from wrongdoing that they did. How wretched was that which they were doing.

verses.arabic: وَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللَّهَ غَافِلًا عَمَّا يَعْمَلُ الظَّالِمُونَ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ تَشْخَصُ فِيهِ الْأَبْصَارُ
verses.translation: And never think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only delays them for a Day when eyes will stare [in horror].

brief_explanation: Zulm (oppression and injustice) is one of the most frequently condemned acts in the Quran, appearing in hundreds of verses as a defining characteristic of those who will be punished. Allah has declared Himself free of injustice and has made it forbidden upon Himself — and He holds tyrants and oppressors to a reckoning where they cannot escape or delay. The Quran describes the Day of Judgment as the day when every oppressor will beg for more time, but there will be none — their deeds sealed and their punishment awaiting. The Prophet (peace be upon him) warned of the 'darkness of zulm' on the Day of Judgment, where oppression becomes literal darkness surrounding those who committed it.
capital_punishment: There is no single hadd for general oppression, but Islamic law provides numerous remedies. Qisas (retaliation) applies when oppression results in bodily harm or murder. The Islamic state is obligated to remove a tyrannical ruler who oppresses his people, and scholars have differed on the conditions for rebellion against an unjust leader. Victims of oppression have the right to seek redress through Islamic courts (qada), and the state must impose ta'zir proportionate to the injustice caused. On the Day of Judgment, Allah will settle all accounts of zulm that were not rectified in this world.
